Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Montréal dbt Meetup
4.5
27 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: Solution BI
🏠Venue Host: Solution BI (4200 Boul. Saint-Laurent suite 425, Montréal)
🍕Food & Beverage: bites and refreshments will be provided

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

5:00 - 5:30 PM | Check in/Registration (30 min)
5:30 - 5:35 PM | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
5:35 - 5:55 PM | Presentation 1 with Audrey Leduc from KOHO Financial (15-20 min)
5:55 - 6:00 PM | Presentation 1 Q&A (5 min)
6:00 - 6:15 PM | Presentation 2 with Daniel Paes from Solution BI (15-20 min)
6:15 - 6:20 PM | Presentation 2 Q&A (5 min)
6:20 - 6:25 PM | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
6:25 - 7:10 PM | Reception (45 min)

🗣️Presentation #1: Audrey Leduc from KOHO Financial
---
🗣️Presentation #2: Daniel Paes from Solution BI

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-montreal channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

Wed, Mar 22, 2023, 6:00 PM to Wed, Mar 22, 2023, 9:00 PM EDT

455 Rue Sainte-Hélène suite 200

455 Rue Sainte-Hélène suite 200 · Montréal, QC

Montréal dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Montréal dbt Meetup

Montréal dbt Meetup
4.5
27 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝 Organizer: Montreal Analytics
🗓️ Date and time: Wednesday, March 22, 2023 from 6PM to 9PM
🏠 Venue: Dialogue @ 455 Sainte-Hélène, Suite 200, Montréal QC H2Y 1T9

🕛 Agenda:
🔹 6 - 6:30PM | Registration and networking
🔹 6:30 - 7PM | Talk #1 + Q&A | Turn your tests into action points with dbt & elementary | Stéphane Burwash, Lead Data Ops Engineer @ Potloc
🔹 7 - 7:30PM | Talk #2 + Q&A | Iterative testing with dbt & data-diff | Will Sweet, Head of Product Experience @ Datafold
🔹 7:30 - 8PM | Talk #3 + Q&A | Enhancing Your Testing Strategy | Hassan Al-Rabea, Lead Data Consultant @ Montreal Analytics
🔹 8 - 9PM | Networking and refreshments 🍻

🎤 Talk #1: Turn your tests into action points with dbt & elementary

Description: Does having a bunch of tests guarantee good data quality? It doesn't (trust us, we know from experience 😞). Join us as we explore how our data team at Potloc turned tests into action items and measurable results using the power of elementary-data. We'll also highlight a couple of the many mistakes we made along the way, and how you can do better than us when implementing your own test suite!
Speaker Bio: With a background in data science and computer engineering, Stéphane is currently the lead Data Ops engineer at Potloc. His responsibilities include creating an maintaining data pipelines, as well as modelling data for use by downstream users. When he's not fixing bugs in production, he enjoys to cook and brew beer.

🎤 Talk #2: Iterative testing with dbt & data-diff

Description: When building dbt models, manual testing is still the most common way to check whether a change to your code had any unintended effects on the data. This process can slow down development and make you less likely to make changes in the future. Join us to learn how open-source data-diff can automate these tests, helping you develop quickly and confidently.
Speaker Bio: As Head of Data and Product Experience at Datafold, Will leads a team of data practitioners and engineers focused on internal analytics and open-source tooling. Prior to Datafold, Will was the Director of Data at Cypress.io, one of the leading test automation tools for modern web applications.

🎤 Talk #3: Enhancing Your Testing Strategy

Description: Join us as we zoom into the world of testing and uncover the vital role it plays in developing a data stack. Discover the difference between verification and validation testing and learn about three types of verification testing: efficacy, style, and performance. We’ll dive deep into efficacy testing, exploring the unique test, relationship test, and even an example with mock data. 📣 Technical folks or people who manage a team and need inspiration to help with their team’s efficiency, make sure to tune in!
Speaker Bio: Passionate about everything entrepreneurship, data & analytics engineering, Hassan is a Lead Data Consultant at Montreal Analytics. He loves empowering companies to take data-driven decisions and helping them with their analytics solutions. As a co-organizer of Montreal Startup Drinks, Hassan enjoys connecting with like-minded people and sharing his love of tech and startups.

🎟️ Special door prizes: We will be giving away 2 one-year licenses to any JetBrains IDE, such as PyCharm or DataGrip, courtesy of JetBrains.

***
To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb
➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-montreal channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.
Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Coalesce: The Analytics Engineering Conference
Network event

183 attendees from 16 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Montréal dbt Meetup
4.5
27 reviews
Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

Hi Montreal dbt community! We are thrilled to bring you the second in-person Montreal dbt meetup. As always, expect to meet other fascinating data folks, learn new things and stay connected to the data community.

👋Organizer: Montreal Analytics
🏠Venue: Dialogue @ 455 Sainte-Hélène, Suite 200 Montréal QC H2Y 1T9
🍕Catering: Drinks & snacks on us!

Agenda
🔹 5:30PM | Doors open. Come, grab a drink and chat!

🔹 6:30 - 7PM | Talk #1 + Q&A | Building sustainable dbt project documentation | Jeremie Pineau, Senior Data Consultant @ Montreal Analytics

🔹 7 - 7:30PM | Talk #2 + Q&A | Building an Optimization Model to Recommend New Store Locations leveraging Modern Data Stack & Open-Source APIs | Maxime Lagresle, Head of Data & Kristi Gourlay, Senior Data Scientist @ Veronica Beard

🔹 7:30-9PM | Networking and refreshments 🍻

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

***

dbt is an open source command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Hiring, Onboarding and Upskilling Analytics Engineers, April 28th 5:30-8:00PM, @ Unito 1265 Rue Berri 7th floor, Montreal, Quebec H2L 4X4

🏠 Host: Montreal Analytics

🗣️Jacob Frackson, Senior Analytics Consultant, Montreal Analytics

Hiring is harder than ever and we feel your pain! Jacob shares Montreal Analytics best practices and tips for hiring and training new Analytics Engineers.

🗣️ Cyril Marques, CEO, Montreal Analytics
Jean-Nicholas Hould, COO, Breathe Life
Sarah Corboliou, Head of Employee Success, Unito
Matthieu Di Mercurio, VP Tech Operations, Dialogue

Finding, training and upskilling Analytics Engineers panel

✨ What to expect at this meetup ✨
5:30 - Welcome Drinks
6:00 - Hiring & Onboarding Best Practices by Jacob Frackson, Senior Analytics Consultant, Montreal Analytics
6:30 - Finding, training and upskilling Analytics Engineers, panel by Cyril Marques, CEO, Montreal Analytics, Matthieu Di Mercurio, VP Tech Operations, Dialogue, Jean-Nicholas Hould, COO, Breathe Life, and Sarah Corboliou, Head of Employee Success, Unito
7:30 - Closing Remarks

Any public space where other people are present holds an inherent risk of exposure to COVID-19 and other communicable diseases. By attending this event, I agree to voluntarily assume all risk related to exposure and agree to not hold dbt Labs or any of their affiliates including partners and sponsors, directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, volunteers (“Representatives”) or venues liable for illness. I understand and agree that dbt Labs has an interest and obligation in protecting the health and well-being of dbt Labs Representatives, venue staff and other attendees; therefore, I agree that, if I’ve chosen not to be fully vaccinated as determined by my local country’s public health guidelines for personal, religious, medical or other reasons, I will only participate in a dbt Labs meetup remotely by watching live or via a recording, and I will not attend in-person. When in attendance in person, I will take necessary precautions while at the event in accordance with local and national health guidelines and regulations, which may include, but be not limited to, engaging in appropriate social distancing, wearing a mask in public areas when not consuming food or beverages, minimizing face touching, frequently washing hands and avoiding risky environments such as overcrowded bars or restaurants. I agree to not attend any dbt Labs event if I feel ill or have had recent exposure to a potentially COVID-19 infected individual. Venue may have other requirements in place, and I agree to abide by those requirements when disclosed by venue.

Dear dbt Community,

In case you missed it, we are hosting Coalesce 2021 from December 6 - 10. This is a FREE online Analytics Engineering Conference where you can connect with over 75 data leaders presenting panels, workshops, activations—and more—exploring today’s analytics engineering ecosystem.

⚠️Please note that 2021 Coalesce won't be hosted on Meetup.com. ✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Subscribe to Coalesce Calendar to automatically add sessions to your personal calendar: https://www.addevent.com/calendar/gL298333

We look forward to connecting with you at Coalesce! ✨
